entries.teachingPoint: Ship English inline in every module bundle; i18next Backend plugin lazy-loads only non-English locales. English requests never hit the server. fallbackLng: 'en' makes every translation failure path resolve to the bundled English resource with zero custom fallback code.
entries.positiveExample: i18n.init({ resources: { en: { 'user-registration': userRegEn, ... } }, fallbackLng: 'en', backend: { loadPath: '/api/translations/{{ns}}/{{lng}}' } }); useTranslation('user-registration');
entries.negativeExample: fetch('/api/translations/user-registration/en').then(r => r.json()).then(setStrings); — server call for English wastes a round trip every session.

entries.teachingPoint: The TranslationResolverService implements a 5-step pipeline: tenant CAS cache → TranslationPolicyGate (CF-812) → marketplace probe → marketplace-delta translation → full tenant translation. The delta path reads marketplace master CAS, diffs against tenant sourceKeys, and only AI-translates the delta — merging with master base.
entries.positiveExample: Step 4 delta-empty: tenant_keys ⊆ marketplace_keys → storeRef(tenantId, moduleId, locale, masterHash) — zero new AI call, tenant reference points at master blob.
entries.negativeExample: Step 4: always translate every tenant key via AI even when marketplace has it — wastes cost and attribution.

entries.teachingPoint: MarketplaceTranslationCacheJob (T667) is the ONLY service permitted to switch CLS context to MASTER_TENANT_ID for translation. Pattern mirrors bootstrap-seeder.service.ts master-tenant switch. All other translation calls use the current ALS context and tenant AI keys. CF-811 iron rule.
entries.positiveExample: await runUnderMasterTenantContext(async () => { const result = await aiProvider.generateStructured(source, schema, { systemPrompt }); await packageContentStore.store(hash, result); await f1523.storeRef({ tenantId: MASTER_TENANT_ID, moduleId, locale, contentHash: hash, sourceHash, connectionType: 'FLOW_SCOPED' }); });
entries.negativeExample: Any service other than T667 calling runUnderMasterTenantContext() for translation — violates CF-811.

entries.teachingPoint: GET /api/translations/:moduleId/:locale MUST return HTTP 200 in all paths. AI failure, policy denial, CAS failure all produce { fallback: true, locale: 'en' }. i18next's native fallbackLng handles the UI side — no error boundary needed on the client.
entries.positiveExample: try { result = await resolver.resolve(...); return sendResult(res, DataProcessResult.success({ translations: result })); } catch (err) { logger.warn('translation failure', err); return sendResult(res, DataProcessResult.success({ fallback: true, locale: 'en' })); }
entries.negativeExample: catch (err) { return res.status(500).json({ error: err.message }); } — propagates 5xx to client, breaks i18next fallback pipeline.

entries.teachingPoint: User preferences store { userId, tenantId, locale, userOverride }. When userOverride === true, tenant changes to i18n.defaultLocale do NOT propagate. When userOverride === false (default), tenant changes do propagate. Pressing 'Reset to tenant default' flips userOverride to false.
entries.negativeExample: effectiveLocale = userPrefs?.locale ?? tenantDefault — ignores userOverride flag; tenant changes silently override user choice.
